title: Pharmaceutical Clinical Trial Data Extraction - Convert PDF Reports to Excel
metaTitle: Clinical Trial Data Extraction - PDF to Excel | GridPull
metaDescription: Extract patient data, adverse events, and efficacy metrics from clinical trial PDFs into structured Excel spreadsheets with AI automation.

hero.headline: Extract Clinical Trial Data from PDFs to Excel Automatically
hero.subheadline: Convert patient records, adverse events, and efficacy data from clinical trial reports into structured spreadsheets for regulatory submissions and analysis

summary: Transform clinical trial PDFs into structured Excel data for regulatory compliance and analysis. Extract patient demographics, adverse events, efficacy endpoints, and safety data from study reports, case report forms, and regulatory documents while maintaining data integrity for FDA submissions.
whoItsFor: Clinical data managers processing study reports
whenThisIsRelevant: Converting case report forms to analyzable datasets
Extracting adverse events for safety databases
Processing efficacy data for regulatory filings
expectedOutputs: Excel files with patient data by row
CSV exports for statistical software import
commonChallenges: Manual transcription errors in patient data
Difficulty tracking adverse events across documents
howItWorksSteps: Upload clinical trial PDFs or scanned forms
Configure fields for patient ID, demographics, and endpoints
AI extracts data maintaining original structure
Review extracted data in Excel format before export
whyPdfExcelAiFits: 99%+ accuracy on clear clinical documents reduces transcription errors
Batch processing handles multiple patient records simultaneously
Custom field selection adapts to different trial protocols
Encrypted processing ensures patient data confidentiality
limitations: Complex multi-page nested tables may require manual review
Heavily redacted patient documents may have missing fields
Handwritten physician notes have limited recognition accuracy

faq.question: Can this extract data from scanned case report forms?
faq.answer: Yes, OCR technology processes scanned PDFs and images of case report forms, though accuracy depends on scan quality and legibility of the original documents.

faq.question: How does this maintain patient data confidentiality?
faq.answer: All files are encrypted during processing and automatically deleted after extraction is complete, ensuring no patient data is permanently stored on servers.

faq.question: What clinical trial data fields can be extracted?
faq.answer: Common fields include patient demographics, adverse events, efficacy endpoints, vital signs, lab values, and safety parameters - customizable based on your study protocol.

faq.question: Does this work with different clinical trial document formats?
faq.answer: The system adapts to various formats including standardized case report forms, study reports, and regulatory documents, though non-standard layouts may need field customization.

trustSignals: Files encrypted and deleted after processing
Batch processing for multiple patient records
Free trial available to test accuracy
exampleUseCases: Extracting adverse events from safety reports for pharmacovigilance databases
Converting patient demographics from case report forms for statistical analysis
Processing efficacy endpoints from study reports for regulatory submissions
Digitizing lab values from scanned forms for data analysis
